title: Target resolver core (keystone)
description:
Build the unified target resolver that every agent-facing command (discover, send, subscribe, get, cancel, download) routes through. This is the keystone of ARD integration — everything else hangs off it.

A 'target' is resolved by form:
- Raw URL (http://, https://) -> direct (current behavior)
- Bare host (acme.com) -> try /.well-known/agent-card.json, then /.well-known/ai-catalog.json
- Local file (./card.json, ./catalog.json) -> read from disk
- ARD URN (urn:air:publisher:ns:name) -> resolve via configured catalog/registry
- name@catalog (assistant@acme) -> resolve from a named catalog
- Bare name (translator) -> search configured registries (may disambiguate)

Requirements:
- Replace/augment the current --service-url with a target concept; keep --service-url working.
- Protocol-neutral internally: entries carry an IANA media type; invocation dispatched by type. A2A entries are invocable; non-A2A (MCP/skill) are resolvable/displayable but clearly 'not invocable here'.
- Respect --output tui/text/json and existing precedence (flags > env > config > defaults).
- Clear errors + Hints when resolution fails or is ambiguous.

title: Add push-notification config commands (push-config create/list/get/delete)
description:
a2acli does not currently expose the A2A TaskPushNotificationConfig surface, which is part of the spec we otherwise test thoroughly. This is the most significant functional/conformance gap.

Add commands mapping to the A2A push notification RPCs:
- push-config create <task-id> <callback-url> [--auth-scheme <scheme>] [--auth-credentials <creds>] — SetTaskPushNotificationConfig
- push-config list <task-id> — ListTaskPushNotificationConfigs
- push-config get <task-id> <config-id> — GetTaskPushNotificationConfig
- push-config delete <task-id> <config-id> — DeleteTaskPushNotificationConfig

Reference: a2a-rs/a2acli implements create/list/delete. Should respect --output tui/text/json and global auth flags. Add e2e conformance coverage against the TCK SUT if it supports push config.

Rationale: completeness is a2acli's differentiator as a spec-testing instrument; this fills a real protocol gap.

title: Add discover/subscribe as primary command names, keep describe/watch as aliases
description: Both issue #1929 and discussion #306 use 'discover' (not 'describe') and 'subscribe' (not 'watch'). Cobra supports aliases via the Aliases field on cobra.Command. Switch to discover/subscribe as primary names and add describe/watch as backwards-compatible aliases. Update help text, README, skills, and conformance tests accordingly.
close_reason: discover/subscribe now primary; describe/watch kept as aliases via Cobra Aliases field

title: Improve Artifact Retrieval UX: Add 'download/retrieve' command, enable saving from 'status', and add --file flag
Add --wait / --sync flag to 'send' command for blocking execution returning only final results
Restructure CLI commands to align semantics (send, watch, get, chat, cancel, config)
Registry search client (search/explore/list)
description:
Dynamic discovery against an ARD Registry REST API. NOTE: this is the v0.9 DRAFT surface — isolate the wire format behind an interface so a spec revision is a localized change.

Commands:
- a2acli search "<query>" [--registry NAME] [--filter type=...,tags=...] [--federation auto|referrals|none] [--page-size N]
    -> POST /search ; render ranked results (score, identifier, type, url, source)
- a2acli explore [--registry NAME] [--facet type] [--facet publisher]   -> POST /explore (facets)
- a2acli registry list <url> [--filter ...] [--order-by ...]            -> GET /agents

Handle the query model (text + filter), federation modes, pagination (pageToken), and referrals. Filter keys are dot-paths into catalog entries. Respect --output.

title: Static catalog support (fetch/list/validate)
description:
Handle ARD static catalogs (the stable, registered layer — no Draft-API risk).

Commands:
- a2acli catalog fetch <url|file>   : GET /.well-known/ai-catalog.json (or read file), display host + entries
- a2acli catalog list <url|file>    : list entries (table for tui/text, array for json)
- a2acli catalog validate <url|file>: validate against ARD JSON Schema + semantic rules (URN format, value-or-reference exclusivity, representativeQueries sizing)

Parse the ai-catalog.json data model: specVersion, host{displayName,identifier}, entries[]{identifier(URN), displayName, type(media type), url XOR data, description, tags, capabilities, representativeQueries, version, trustManifest}. Handle nested catalogs (type application/ai-catalog+json) and inline data vs url.

title: Catalog/registry config + URN resolution
description:
Make catalogs and registries first-class config alongside named environments.

Extend ~/.config/a2acli/config.yaml:
  catalogs: { acme: 'https://acme.com/.well-known/ai-catalog.json' }
  registries: { public: 'https://...', corp: 'https://...' }
  default_registry: corp
  # an env may carry a registry/catalog instead of a service_url
  envs: { corp: { registry: 'https://registry.internal.corp/api/v1/' } }

Commands:
- a2acli catalog add <alias> <url>     : register a catalog alias
- a2acli catalog remove <alias>

Resolution: URN (urn:air:acme.com:agent:assistant) and name@catalog (assistant@acme) resolve through configured catalogs to an agent card URL, then hand off to the resolver core. Wire into resolver core target forms.

title: ARD (Agentic Resource Discovery) integration
description:
Incorporate the Agentic Resource Discovery spec (https://github.com/ards-project/ard-spec) so a2acli becomes a single tool for BOTH discovery and interaction of A2A agents (and, longer term, other agentic resources).

Design north star: discovery and use merge via a smart TARGET RESOLVER, not a parallel command silo. Every command that takes an agent target accepts richer forms (URL, host, file, ARD URN, name@catalog, bare name) and the resolver figures out the rest. Catalogs/registries become first-class config alongside named environments. The existing Bubble Tea TUI becomes a discovery browser (discover -> select -> view card -> converse).

Phasing is risk-aware: ARD is v0.9 Draft (IANA media types pending, wire format may change), so build the stable static-catalog + resolver foundation first and isolate the Draft registry REST API behind an interface.

Design doc: docs/ARD_INTEGRATION.md
Naming note: as scope expands beyond A2A (MCP servers, skills in catalogs), the resolver/catalog core is kept protocol-neutral so an eventual rename (e.g. 'agents') is packaging, not rearchitecture.

title: Add discover --extended for authenticated extended agent card
description:
Add a --extended flag to 'discover' that fetches the authenticated extended AgentCard via the A2A GetAuthenticatedExtendedCard RPC, rather than the public well-known card.

  a2acli discover --extended --token <TOKEN>
  a2acli discover --extended --auth 'Bearer ...'

Behavior:
- Requires auth (token/auth headers); error with a clear Hint if none provided.
- Falls back to / errors clearly if the agent does not advertise support for an extended card.
- Respects --output tui/text/json.

Reference: a2a-rs/a2acli supports requesting an extended card. Small, spec-relevant, useful for testing agents that expose richer cards to authenticated callers.

title: Support reading message from stdin on send
description:
Allow piping a message into 'send' via stdin, matching ericabouaf/a2a-cli ergonomics:

  echo 'test message' | a2acli send
  cat prompt.txt | a2acli send

Behavior:
- If no positional message arg is given AND stdin is not a TTY, read the full message text from stdin.
- If both a positional arg and piped stdin are present, positional wins (or document precedence clearly).
- Works with --output json/text/tui and --wait/--immediate.

Rationale: natural fit for the scripting/agent story and test pipelines. Low effort, high ergonomic win. Currently only --instruction-file reads from a file; raw stdin is the missing piece.

title: Add --output flag with tui/text/json modes
description:
Add a --output <mode> global flag supporting three output modes: 'tui' (default, interactive Bubble Tea), 'text' (non-interactive human-readable, no animations, for CI/piped output), 'json' (machine-readable NDJSON, equivalent to current -n/--no-tui).

Design decisions:
- Long form only (--output), no -o shorthand — -o is already taken by --out-dir on send/get/watch/download
- Keep -n/--no-tui as a backward-compatible alias for --output json (do not remove)
- NO_COLOR=true and A2ACLI_NO_TUI=true should map to --output text rather than --output json (plain human-readable, not machine-readable)
- 'text' mode is the non-interactive human experience: formatted output, no spinners/animations — equivalent to what git does when stdout is not a terminal

Aligns with the --output/-o flag proposed in a2aproject/A2A#1929 and a2aproject/a2a-go discussion #306. The -o shorthand conflict is a known gap vs those proposals.

title: Add multi-modal message input to send (--parts, --json, --file, --data)
description: Discussion #306 specifies two flags for non-text message input: --json <body> accepts a raw JSON Message object, --parts <json> accepts a raw JSON parts array (CLI wraps it in a ROLE_USER Message). Also -f <file> to read a Message from a JSON file. Currently a2acli only accepts plain text as a positional argument, limiting use with multi-modal agents (file URLs, data parts, etc.).

title: Add send --immediate flag (fire-and-forget)
description: Both issue #1929 (--return-immediately) and discussion #306 (--immediate) specify a fire-and-forget mode for send: set ReturnImmediately in SendMessageConfig and return the Task ID immediately without waiting for completion or streaming. The user can then use 'get' or 'watch' to follow up. Currently a2acli only supports blocking (--wait) or streaming (default).
close_reason: Added --immediate flag to send; sets ReturnImmediately:true and returns task ID immediately

title: Add --timeout global flag
description: Discussion #306 specifies a global --timeout <dur> flag with a default of 30s. Currently a2acli has no request timeout, which means hung agents block indefinitely. Add as a persistent flag on rootCmd, apply to all HTTP/gRPC client calls. Should accept Go duration strings (e.g. 30s, 2m).
close_reason: Implemented in v1.4.4: --timeout duration flag applied to getResolver() HTTP client

title: Implement serve --exec mode
description: Discussion #306 specifies an exec mode for 'serve': wrap any subprocess as an A2A agent. Message text goes to stdin, stdout becomes the response artifact. Exit code 0 = TASK_STATE_COMPLETED, non-zero = TASK_STATE_FAILED. Supports a --chunk=<delimiter> flag for streaming output incrementally as ArtifactUpdateEvents. The flag exists in code but is marked 'not yet supported'.

title: Implement serve --proxy mode
description: Discussion #306 specifies a proxy mode for 'serve': forward all requests to an upstream A2A agent, log traffic to stderr, inject service parameters and auth headers. The flag exists in code but is marked 'not yet supported'. Implementation should create an a2aclient.Client for the upstream, forward each A2A operation, inject --svc-param values via AttachServiceParams, and derive the proxy's own AgentCard from the upstream card with the local address substituted.

title: Update a2a-go SDK to latest version and fix tests
Refactor colorizedHelpFunc to use semantic tokens instead of hardcoded ANSI/lipgloss colors
Implement adaptive/theme-aware coloring for optimal contrast in both Light and Dark terminal modes
Audit a2acli help and TUI output to maximize data-ink ratio (remove non-essential color/borders)
Introduce semantic color tokens (Accent, Muted, Pass, Warn, Fail) for consistent styling
Audit and synchronize flag short-names across all commands
Standardize 'Hint' guidance for common error states (missing config, invalid IDs)
Implement basic semantic help styling (colorized headers and flags)
Add 'Long' descriptions and 'Example' blocks to all core commands
Organize a2acli commands into logical groups (Tasks, Info, System)
Update README.md documenting how --wait/--sync maps to A2A SendMessage Blocking:true parameter
Update README.md with new CLI command semantics (send, watch, get)
Implement 'cancel' command to terminate tasks
Configuration Management (XDG + Named Server Environments, --config)
Phase 3: Migrate a2acli to use the a2a-go SDK release/spec-v1 branch
Phase 2: Add TCK SUT to Makefile and implement baseline conformance tests using os/exec
Phase 1: Add non-interactive mode (--no-tui) and JSON output for CLI testability
Add golangci-lint configured to align with Google Go standards
In addition to .env config, let's use XDG base directory configs
ARD conformance testing (catalog + registry)
description:
a2acli's signature move applied to ARD: be the Go-native conformance tester for ARD manifests and registries (mirrors how it already tests A2A v0.3/v1.0).

Commands:
- a2acli conformance catalog <url|file> : validate ai-catalog.json vs ARD JSON Schema + semantic rules
- a2acli conformance registry <url>     : probe POST /search, POST /explore, GET /agents for spec compliance (status codes, pagination envelope, score range, entry structure)

Reference ARD's own conformance tool (Python) and schemas (CDDL/JSON Schema/OpenAPI) for parity. Add e2e coverage. Depends on static catalog + registry client.

title: TUI discovery browser (discover -> select -> converse)
description:
Turn the existing Bubble Tea TUI into an interactive discovery browser — the daily-driver experience that no other community CLI has.

Flow: 'a2acli search "translation agent"' (no --output) renders ranked results in the TUI; arrow-select an entry; view its AgentCard (skills, security, transports); then act inline ('Send a message?') which streams the response in the same session.

This is the discovery HALF that composes with the conversation/REPL half (a2ac-srx). Together they form discover -> select -> view -> converse without leaving the tool. Depends on resolver core + registry search.

Coordinate scope with a2ac-srx (chat REPL): the browser handles discover+select; a2ac-srx handles the multi-turn conversation loop after selection.

title: Local cache / known-agents store
description:
Bridge discovery -> repeated use by caching resolved cards locally (XDG cache dir), keyed by URN. Mirrors agntcy .a2aagents/ and spec-works install model.

Commands:
- a2acli catalog pull <alias>   : fetch + cache all entries from a catalog for fast/offline use
- a2acli agents                 : list locally-known (cached) agents
- resolver checks cache first for URN/name targets -> instant, offline-capable

Include cache invalidation (updatedAt/ETag where available) and a way to clear the cache.

title: Add list tasks filters: --context, --status, --since, --with-artifacts
description: Discussion #306 specifies richer filtering for 'list tasks': --context <id> (filter by context ID), --status <state> (filter by task state, short forms: submitted/working/completed/failed/canceled/rejected), --since <time> (RFC 3339, only tasks updated after this timestamp), --with-artifacts (include artifacts in response), --history <n> (include history messages per task). Currently a2acli only supports --limit and --page-token.

title: Expose A2A CLI as an MCP server via STDIO (--mcp)
Support dynamic transport selection (gRPC vs JSONRPC) based on AgentCard SupportedInterfaces
Interactive Chat Mode REPL using Bubbletea
serve mock ARD catalog + registry
description:
Extend 'serve' so a2acli can stand up the full ARD test topology with no external deps:
- a2acli serve --catalog <file>  : host a static /.well-known/ai-catalog.json
- a2acli serve --registry <file> : mock registry exposing POST /search, POST /explore, GET /agents over an index

Combined with 'serve --echo' (the agent under test), a single binary plays all three roles: agent + catalog + registry. Enables a self-contained discover->resolve->invoke e2e conformance fixture. Depends on static catalog + registry client knowledge.

title: Explore persistent auth / token storage integrated with environments
description:
EXPLORATORY (not committed). spec-works/a2a-ask has the richest auth of any community CLI: OAuth2 device-code + client-credentials flows, encrypted token storage (DPAPI on Windows, 0600 files elsewhere), auto-refresh, per-issuer client registration, and per-tenant token separation.

a2acli already has named environment profiles (--env) and a config file. Persistent auth could COMPLEMENT environments: e.g. store a token/credential per named environment so 'a2acli send --env prod' transparently authenticates.

Open questions to resolve before committing:
- Is persistent SSO actually needed for a TESTING tool, or is per-invocation --token/--auth sufficient?
- Scope: just token storage keyed to environments, or full OAuth2 device-code/client-credentials flows?
- Security: how to store credentials safely cross-platform without a heavy dependency.

Decision: keep as p4 backlog/idea until a concrete need appears. The simpler, higher-value adjacent win is making 'discover' surface security schemes clearly (helps users know WHAT auth an agent needs) — partially done today, could be polished. See also a2ac-o2i (discover --extended).

Reference: spec-works/a2a-ask auth login/logout/status, register-client/list-clients/remove-client.
